stories.headline: ‘Encouraging progress’ made as first round US-Iran talks end
stories.summary: The first round of US-Iran talks has ended with both sides agreeing on a roadmap towards a final deal.

stories.headline: Lower-level technical talks to continue as first round of direct US-Iran negotiations wrap up
stories.summary: Iran’s top diplomat hailed mediators Pakistan and Qatar’s efforts  over what he called “major progress” to end the continued Israeli offensive on Lebanon, despite a fragile truce. The comments came after Iran and the US kickstarted a 6-day negotiating window to permanently end the war.

stories.headline: First round of US-Iran talks ends with 'encouraging progress', mediators say
stories.summary: The US-Iran talks began on Sunday in Switzerland, after last week's agreement, which includes a commitment to reach a final deal within 60 days.

stories.headline: US-Iran Talks Progress, Europe Shifts Strategy | Insight with Haslinda Amin 06/22/2026
stories.summary: Insight with Haslinda Amin, a daily news program featuring in-depth, high-profile interviews and analysis to give viewers the complete picture on the stories that matter. The show features prominent leaders spanning the worlds of business, finance, politics and culture. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Oil In Focus As US-Iran Peace Talks Show Signs of Progress | The Asia Trade 6/22/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Oil prices slip as progress in US-Iran talks eases supply concerns
stories.summary: Oil prices retreated on Monday after signs of progress in US-Iran negotiations eased fears of ongoing supply disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil transit route.

stories.headline: More than half of France under red alerts as heatwave intensifies
stories.summary: The health minister warns many citizens "will suffer", with temperatures set to peak on Monday.

stories.headline: Thai Shipper Remains Wary of Hormuz Despite Ceasefire
stories.summary: Bangkok-based Precious Shipping Pcl, whose vessel was struck in March by Iranian projectiles in the Strait of Hormuz, said it remains cautious about sending ships through one of the world’s most important energy and trade chokepoints.

stories.headline: What We Know About the Los Angeles Warehouse Fire
stories.summary: Smoke spread across Los Angeles on Sunday, but the Fire Department said it was making progress in extinguishing the fire at the cold-storage warehouse. State and local officials have declared an emergency.

stories.headline: US and Iran meet for ‘tense’ but ‘constructive’ ceasefire talks
stories.summary: Negotiators from the US and Iran wrapped a day of talks in Switzerland.

stories.headline: Europe Today: Starmer set to step down as US-Iran talks and Moldova summit unfold
stories.summary: US-Iran talks continued after a temporary pause threatened the fragile agreement, as Brussels hosted the second EU-Moldova summit focused on Chişinău's membership bid. Meanwhile, pressure grew on UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer as tensions between Poland and Ukraine continued to escalate.

stories.headline: Trump-backed political outsider wins Colombia election, initial count shows
stories.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella appears to have narrowly defeated his rival Iván Cepeda, who says the preliminary count is "not yet official or binding".

stories.headline: Rightwing populist ‘El Tigre’ wins Colombia election
stories.summary: Trump-loving political outsider Abelardo de la Espriella narrowly beats leftist continuity candidate

stories.headline: Chinese Steel Market Enters Long Plateau After Property Crash
stories.summary: The Chinese steel market is navigating a prolonged weakening in demand rather than a cliff-drop collapse, as the steep decline from property is increasingly offset by manufacturing and exports, according to experts at an industry conference last week.

stories.headline: Chinese Stocks in Hong Kong Near Bear Market | The China Show 6/22/2026
stories.summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and Avril Hong give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Embattled Superintendent of Los Angeles School District Resigns
stories.summary: Alberto Carvalho resigned Sunday, months after the F.B.I. raided his home and office. He came to Los Angeles from Miami with a reputation for raising student test scores but also made missteps.

stories.headline: Clearance rates hit six-year low as more than half of Australian homes up for auction fail to sell

stories.headline: China crackdown rattles Hong Kong wealth hub status
Newsletter: A Commissioner's secret trip to Israel
stories.summary: In this newsletter: A high-ranking EU official visits Jerusalem on Monday, exposing a vacuum of information about the trip; UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer is expected to lay-out how he will vacate Number 10 Downing Street; plus, the EU-Moldova summit gets underway in the Belgian capital.

stories.headline: China's import of custard apples is sparking fears in Taiwan
stories.summary: Taiwan's agriculture ministry is worried that Beijing wants to weaponise a local specialty: the atemoya.

stories.headline: Why sinodollars outweigh the petroyuan
stories.summary: China’s currency is becoming a more important part of the global financial system but the dollar is still in control

stories.headline: Starmer on brink of quitting as UK prime minister
stories.summary: Labour leader is under intense pressure to set out timetable for transition of power

stories.headline: Ireland says it can secure an EU capital markets deal this year
stories.summary: Taoiseach Micheál Martin tells FT there are ‘ways of landing this’

stories.headline: EasyJet Rejects Three Separate Overtures From Castlelake
stories.summary: EasyJet Plc has rejected three separate offers from Castlelake LP, prompting the investment firm to take its latest proposal that values the budget carrier at about £4.74 billion ($6.3 billion) directly to shareholders.

stories.headline: Chinese Stocks in Hong Kong Rebound From Edge of Bear Market
stories.summary: A gauge of Chinese equities in Hong Kong trimmed losses, supported by a rotation into financial shares as investors shifted out of artificial intelligence-linked names.

stories.headline: Bond Traders Finally Get Single View of Activity in UK Market
stories.summary: Bond investors gained access to a single stream of UK trading data after a transparency initiative that’s been years in the making went live.

stories.headline: Korea Mulls Steps to Rein In Leveraged Samsung, SK Hynix ETFs
stories.summary: South Korean authorities are weighing measures to curb risks from leveraged single-stock exchange-traded funds tracking Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc., reflecting concern over an asset class whose popularity has surged with the AI boom.

stories.headline: Pound Trades Near 2026 Low as UK Political Uncertainty Builds
stories.summary: The pound traded near this year’s low on expectations Keir Starmer will set out a timetable for his departure as UK prime minister in coming days.

stories.headline: Asset Manager CSOP Lifts Options Cap for SK Hynix Fund
stories.summary: CSOP Asset Management Ltd. is lifting a ceiling on the use of options in a $14.4 billion leveraged exchange-traded fund linked to South Korean chipmaker SK Hynix Inc., giving the fund manager more flexibility after a wild rally in the company’s shares.

stories.headline: Chinese AI Stocks Rally on Demand Optimism and Policy Support
stories.summary: Chinese artificial intelligence-related stocks jumped, with sentiment boosted by a more supportive policy tone from Beijing and ongoing global demand for the technology.

stories.headline: Far-right millionaire wins Colombia’s razor-tight presidential election
stories.summary: Leftwing opponent alleges vote count irregularities after Trump-endorsed lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella secures narrow majorityThe Trump-admiring far-right millionaire lawyer and self-styled “outsider” Abelardo de la Espriella has won Colombia’s presidential runoff, defeating the leftwing senator Iván Cepeda.With 99.99% of ballots counted in the preliminary vote tally, De la Espriella had secured 12.96m votes, or 49.66%, just 250,830 more than C

stories.summary: Follow today’s news liveGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastHume has no problem with more debate on NDIS changes, but criticises ‘horse trading’ between Labor and the GreensJane Hume is speaking to RN Breakfast now, saying the Coalition has no problem with increased scrutiny over changes to the NDIS should the Greens negotiate a longer inquiry into the scheme the week.I suppose one of our concerns is that perhaps the change

stories.headline: Almost three tonnes of cocaine found buried under Sydney property in Australia’s biggest ever seizure, police say
stories.summary: Australian federal police arrested and charged two men after allegedly finding $800m worth of cocaine in ‘bunkers’ under shipping containersFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastPolice claim to have made Australia’s biggest ever cocaine bust after finding $800m worth of the drug buried under false flooring on a semi-rural property.Two men, aged 21 and 25, allegedly tried t

stories.headline: New York City welcomes the summer solstice with Times Square yoga
stories.summary: New Yorkers gathered for the 24th annual "Solstice in Times Square” event to celebrate the summer solstice.

stories.headline: Colombia presidential candidate De la Espriella celebrates preliminary lead
stories.summary: Colombian outsider Abelardo de la Espriella in an armoured vehicle next to his vice-presidential candidate.

stories.headline: Colombia’s De La Espriella claims victory on preliminary results
stories.summary: Right-wing candidate Abelardo de la Espriella has claimed victory in Colombia's presidential election.

stories.headline: Ebola closure cuts off a lifeline between DRC’s Goma and Rwanda
stories.summary: Health officials defend the restrictions, but traders say the measures have disrupted the flow of goods and income.

stories.headline: China's EV makers BYD, Xpeng move SUVs upmarket but risk glut
ByteDance sidelines listing as China's first $1 trillion valuation nears
U.S. Steel CEO says 'golden share' won't curb reform strategy
Summer Davos, BRICS security meeting, Bangkok election

stories.summary: Fuel had already been rationed due to shortages caused by Kyiv's attacks against supply routes in Russian-occupied territories.

stories.headline: Oil Declines After US-Iran Peace Talks Show Signs of Progress
stories.summary: Oil dropped following signs of progress in peace talks between Washington and Tehran, which appeared to get off to a rocky start after US President Donald Trump issued a fresh threat against Iran.

stories.headline: Neither the War Nor Trump’s Deal Terminated the Main Threats in Iran, Analysts Say
stories.summary: Neither the war nor the agreement terminated the main threats emanating from Iran, many analysts said.

stories.headline: Football fans in Gaza gather to watch Spain-Saudi Arabia match
stories.summary: Palestinians in Gaza gathered to watch Spain's World Cup victory over Saudi Arabia.

stories.headline: Stocks Drift, Oil Falls After US-Iran Peace Talks: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Stocks in the US and Europe struggled for direction, finding little support from lower oil prices following signs of diplomatic headway between the US and Iran.

stories.headline: Bolivian president declares state of emergency and deploys military to quell anti-government protests
stories.summary: Bulldozers sent in to clear roadblocks that have stifled the country as farmers and Indigenous groups protest against conservative president Bolivia’s president declared a state of emergency on Saturday and deployed soldiers and bulldozers to raze anti-government roadblocks that have paralysed the country.For more than six weeks, unions, Indigenous groups and coca farmers have marched through cities and blocked roads across the country with rubbl

stories.headline: US and Iran publicly trade threats as talks take place in Switzerland
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump on Sunday threatened to strike Iran if it did not stop Hezbollah from "causing trouble", while Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf warned the US to "be careful with their statements".

stories.headline: Starmer reflecting on 'political realities' amid reports of imminent resignation
stories.summary: The British PM has repeatedly vowed to fight on in his role but has come under increasing pressure in recent weeks.

stories.headline: Currency crash and visa crackdowns force Indian students to rethink studying abroad
stories.summary: A weaker rupee and tougher immigration rules are pushing Indian students away from popular destinations like the US and UK.

stories.headline: Starmer mulls podium moment after Chequers retreat
stories.summary: Prime minister goes ‘dark’ on colleagues as he spends weekend discussing his political future with wife, Victoria

stories.headline: Soaring Profits in Emerging Markets Build Case for a Raging Bull Market
stories.summary: For the first time in four years, companies in emerging markets are beating profit estimates, giving investors a fresh reason to believe the bull market is just getting started.

stories.headline: Protect Every Animal From Cruelty? Not in 2026, Oregon Democrats Say
stories.summary: A possible referendum in Oregon on animal rights would end fishing, hunting, even pest control, just when Democrats are trying really hard not to be seen as “weirdos again.”

stories.headline: Trump Administration Shuttered a Criminal Probe Into Fraudster’s Clemency
stories.summary: Federal prosecutors had been examining the circumstances behind the commutation of David Gentile’s sentence. He was aided by a Catholic priest friendly with the president.

stories.headline: For cash-strapped farmers, deal to end Iran fighting comes too late
stories.summary: Some of the president’s strongest supporters are hurting as midterm elections approach.

stories.headline: Japan fans celebrate 4-0 World Cup win in Tokyo
stories.summary: Fans gathered at Tokyo's Shibuya Crossing after Japan beat Tunisia 4-0 in a World Cup group-stage match.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill six people in Gaza including Al Jazeera cameraman, officials say
stories.summary: The Israeli military accused Ahmed Wishah of being a "Hamas sniper operative", without providing evidence.

stories.headline: Zelensky returns highest Polish honour after award stripped
stories.summary: Ukraine's president said his country was open to "engagement" about "difficult and painful chapters of our shared past".

stories.headline: Lebanese turtle conservationist Mona Khalil killed by Israeli strike
stories.summary: Mona Khalil, who had refused to leave the beach she had spent years protecting, died after several days in hospital, an environmental group said.

stories.headline: Bolivian president declares state of emergency
stories.summary: The move comes after weeks of anti-government protests that have caused a shortage of basic goods in Bolivia.

stories.headline: Israel and Hezbollah continue strikes despite ceasefire agreement
stories.summary: At least 20 people have reportedly been killed by Israeli air strikes as the Israeli military said Hezbollah had fired at Israeli troops in southern Lebanon.

stories.headline: Meloni tells Trump to 'focus on your own popularity' as row escalates
stories.summary: The US president earlier questioned Meloni's popularity after suggesting she "begged" for a photo at G7 summit

stories.headline: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
stories.summary: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.

stories.headline: 3 Hikers Die at Grand Canyon Amid ‘Dangerous’ High Temperatures
stories.summary: The hikers died at Grand Canyon National Park in separate episodes as temperatures peaked above 100 degrees, the authorities said.

stories.headline: Thousands killed in US-Israeli war on Iran - but experts say true total may never be known
stories.summary: Experts say internet, media and government restrictions have all hampered casualty reporting across the region.

stories.headline: Global framework for reparatory justice adopted at landmark Ghana conference

stories.headline: US-Iran deal leaves core sticking points unresolved - and a $300bn question
stories.summary: Trump has insisted the deal ensures that Iran will never buy, develop or produce a nuclear weapon. But text of the agreement falls short of that.

stories.headline: What Iran and US get from deal and why both could struggle to keep it
stories.summary: BBC analysts assess the claims by both Washington and Tehran to have won a victory with the deal to end their war.

stories.headline: Suspected gang leader shot dead in flower bouquet ambush at airport
stories.summary: The gunmen concealed their weapons behind flowers and stuffed toys while waiting for the victim outside Guayaquil airport.

stories.headline: Bolivia signs $20m deal with US to fight drug trafficking
stories.summary: Under its new president, Bolivia is re-establishing severed ties with the US to combat organised crime.

stories.headline: How to launch a tech product
stories.summary: Create a slick, irony-free corporate safe space

stories.headline: Tata’s big bets are yet to pay off
stories.summary: The Indian conglomerate’s boardroom drama highlights a speculative strategy

stories.headline: Barbados prime minister announces manifesto for slavery reparations
stories.summary: Updated document, which emphasises harm done to African women, is being considered by other Caribbean countriesBarbados’s prime minister, Mia Mottley, has announced a new manifesto from Caribbean leaders asserting the “moral, ethical and legal case” for reparations over damage caused by hundreds of years of enslavement.Mottley was speaking at a “historic” conference in Ghana to advance the push for reparatory justice after the United Nations adop

stories.headline: 'It was surreal': British couple describe having warning shots fired near them by Russian warship
stories.summary: The retired couple tell BBC Newsnight they tried to show the warship they had changed course in the English Channel before the shots were fired.

stories.headline: Dáil passes abortion bill to remove three-day wait
stories.summary: The proposal is to remove the three-day wait for an abortion in early pregnancy in the Republic of Ireland.

stories.headline: Tournament of losers
stories.summary: The World Cup is a festival for corporate has-beens

stories.headline: Lululemon apologises after Japanese drum row at Great Wall yoga event
stories.summary: Online uproar follows Canadian brand’s use of taiko drum at sponsored festival held to celebrate Chinese cultureThe activewear brand Lululemon has apologised after a promotional event held on the Great Wall of China appeared to mistakenly feature a Japanese drum, prompting an uproar.The Canadian-headquartered company, known for its upmarket leggings, has been growing rapidly in China and arranged for a yoga festival to take place in late May on a

stories.headline: Hungary's MPs block return of Orbán, limiting rule of PM to eight years
stories.summary: The vote fulfils a promise by new PM Péter Magyar to stop his predecessor from becoming prime minister again.

stories.headline: Bowen: Iran deal ends Trump's war that revealed limit of US dominance
stories.summary: The deal to end fighting and re-open the Strait of Hormuz leaves the sides where they were 24 hours before the war - only with thousands now dead.

stories.headline: What one country's experiment says about attempts to boost birth rates
stories.summary: Why did Hungary’s pronatalist approach deliver an early rise in births only then to fall back? And what lessons does it offer to other countries desperate to lift fertility?

stories.headline: Dublin-born Cape Verde star recruited on LinkedIn gets World Cup chance
stories.summary: They are one of the smallest countries to play in a World Cup, but Cape Verde are aiming high, with a Dublin-born defender who used to work in a bank among their ranks.
